actionable_feedback: Fix the execution-claim inconsistency: update the grader text so it accurately states whether an execution occurred. If the verification relied on a separate execution recorded in the trace, say so explicitly (e.g., "verification used a recorded python execution step from the agent trace"). If no execution was actually run, remove the claim that derived pressures were executed.

Resolve semantic-scoring inconsistency: either remove semantic_score / semantic_correctness_score fields (set to null) if semantic evaluation was not performed, or document the basis for semantic scoring (cite the trace step(s) and artifact checks used) and keep the scores. Ensure deterministic_precheck notes reflect the chosen stance.

Correct or remove the unsupported numeric mitigation example. If the intent was to show the controlling pressure after applying the documented mitigated target (0.90  limit), compute and present the ratio consistently (e.g., mitigated_controlling_ratio = mitigated_target / original_limit). If the CSV intentionally reports sensitivity-case ratios instead, clarify the distinction in lab_plan.md.

Add the suggested CSV enhancement: include an explicit per-record numeric column named mitigated_controlling_value (the controlling metric's ratio after applying the mitigation target) so reviewers can immediately verify that mitigation reduces the controlling pressure below 1.0 where intended.

Add a short expert_review_memo.md (or an 'Expert review' section in lab_plan.md clearly labeled) that separates the human-judgment narrative from reproducible logic. Include the justification for marking R4 as needs_review and any caveats for applying the mitigation targets.
Rerun deterministic prechecks and update grader/verifier metadata to reflect the corrected statements and any newly added columns/artifacts, then publish the revised attempt (478-revised).
artifact_specific_comments: beamforming_fault_localization.csv: contains required fields and per-record calculations; add mitigated_controlling_value (numeric ratio after applying mitigation target) to make mitigation impact explicit.
lab_plan.md: preserves formulas and reproducible logic; add a short, clearly labeled 'Expert review' section summarizing human judgment for R4 and any caveats.
evidence_refs: Trace summary JSON: trace steps compute_record_localization and write_deliverables
feedback_summary: Deliverables are present and calculations are reproducible; per-record pressures, sensitivity case, mitigation targets, budget, and needs_review flags are all included. However, the grader report contains three internal inconsistencies: (1) it claims the provided Python snippet was not executed while the trace shows a python execution step that produced the same per-record pressures; (2) it simultaneously claims semantic correctness was not evaluated and also assigns semantic_score/semantic_correctness_score = 1.0; (3) it contains an unsupported numeric illustrative statement about mitigation (example converting R1 -> 0.9) that does not match values present in the CSV previews. Addressing these issues will improve clarity, reproducibility, and trustworthiness.

extra_model_fields.recommended_next_action: Implement the fixes listed above, add mitigated_controlling_value to CSV, include an expert review memo, and rerun prechecks. Produce 478-revised.

verifier_issues_summary.short_description: Grader claims it did not execute the provided Python snippet, but the agent trace shows a python execution step that computed the same per-record pressures.
verifier_issues_summary.suggested_fix: Remove or correct the limitation claiming no execution; if execution was performed separately, attribute the verification to the trace-execution.

verifier_issues_summary.short_description: Grader simultaneously states semantic correctness was not evaluated yet assigns a semantic score of 1.0.
verifier_issues_summary.suggested_fix: Clarify whether semantic grading was performed. If it was, cite the trace/steps used; if not, remove or nullify semantic score fields.

verifier_issues_summary.short_description: Grader gives an unsupported numeric illustrative mitigation example (R1 -> 0.9) not matched by artifact values.
verifier_issues_summary.suggested_fix: Correct the numeric example or show the calculation that maps mitigated target to the reported controlling ratio.
